entries.name: CLIENT_GetLocalDataContext
entries.notes: Returns the local client data-table context. SKILLTREE_CanAllocateSkill calls it first and passes the result to CLIENT_GetLocalPlayer. It was used by the superseded mono-rank BulkSkillPointAllocation queue and is not required by 1.2.1.

entries.name: CLIENT_GetLocalPlayer
entries.notes: Returns the local player for a client data context. The 92777 skill-tree eligibility path calls it at 0x14C3DB6. It was used by the superseded mono-rank BulkSkillPointAllocation queue and is not required by 1.2.1.

entries.name: CLIENT_SendFiveByteCommandPacket
entries.notes: Builds a five-byte client command from (opcode byte, value word, extra word) and submits it through the outgoing queue at 0xEE2A0. For opcode 0x3B, value is the skill id and extra encodes additional ranks: 0 means one total, 4 means five total, and 0xFFFF means every rank the runtime permits. The 0/0xFFFF client branches are proven at 0x14C6A11/0x14C6A01; extra=4 producing exactly five instant ranks was validated in game on 92777. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 hooks the strict 29-byte prologue and filters only opcode 0x3B.

entries.name: CLIENT_QueueOutgoingPacket
entries.notes: Generic outgoing client packet queue called by the five-byte builder at 0xEC700. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 preserves this native route and sends exactly one native 0x3B request per Ctrl or confirmed Shift action.

entries.name: SKILLS_GetRuntimeMaxLevel
entries.notes: Returns the effective runtime maximum for a compiled SkillsTxt row and data context. SKILLTREE_CanAllocateSkill calls it at 0x14C3E54. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 delegates the cap to the authoritative native bulk handler and no longer calls this helper.

entries.name: ITEMS_BuildItemTooltip
entries.notes: Builds the final item-tooltip string. Transmogrify 1.0.3 hooks its strict 32-byte prologue, calls the native builder first, then inserts a bounded red localized conversion hint below required-stat lines when the loaded ItemsTxt row enables Transmogrify. Cold-start and visual placement were confirmed in game on 2026-07-21.

entries.name: ITEMS_GetStatsDescription
entries.notes: Builds the item-stat description buffer used by the tooltip. AdvancedItemTooltips hooks its strict 32-byte prologue, calls vanilla first, then appends only bounded presentation text. Four direct call sites and the item-type branch were verified in the persistent 92777 workbench.

entries.name: STATLIST_GetUnitBaseStat
entries.notes: Generic unit base-stat getter with ABI (unit, stat id, 16-bit layer). DurabilityResistance hooks its max-durability calls; SKILLTREE_CanAllocateSkill calls it with stat 5 and layer 0 for unspent skill points. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.0.7 used it for its superseded per-rank acknowledgement queue; 1.2.1 no longer reads it.

entries.name: STATLIST_UnitGetStatValue
entries.notes: Unit-level (unit, statId, layer) wrapper with 91 direct callers. It packs the key as (statId << 16) | layer, resolves ItemStatCost, then calls the full-stat lookup at 0x2F9B10. EnhancedDamageMinMaxFix 1.0.0 hooked this wrapper unsuccessfully.

entries.name: STATLIST_EvaluateStatOperations
entries.notes: Recursive ItemStatCost operation evaluator. Its 14-entry switch maps operation 13 to 0x2F9709, where an item-owned stat is combined with the configured op-stat value as a percentage.

entries.name: STATLIST_GetBaseStatValue
entries.notes: Reads the packed stat from the base Stats array at stat-list offset 0x30 and applies the ItemStatCost minimum clamp.

entries.name: STATLIST_GetTotalStatValue
entries.notes: Reads the packed stat from FullStats at offset 0xA8 for an extended list, or from base Stats at offset 0x30 otherwise. It is a lookup, not the recursive ItemStatCost evaluator. EnhancedDamageMinMaxFix 1.1.0 hooked this function unsuccessfully and also decoded the packed stat in reverse.

entries.name: STATLIST_UpdateUnitStat
entries.notes: Updates a computed FullStats entry and runs the native unit/callback propagation path. ABI proven from calls at 0x2FA476, 0x2FA4B1 and 0x2FA7DD: (statList, packedStat, value, itemStatCost, callbackUnit).

entries.name: STATLIST_EvaluateAndUpdateStat
entries.notes: Evaluates a packed stat through 0x2F8AD0, recursively refreshes dependent op stats, and normally commits through 0x2F9DB0. Its operation switch at 0x2FA697 maps op=13 to 0x2FA8C7, which suppresses the commit when statList ownerType at +0x08 is UNIT_ITEM (4). EnhancedDamageMinMaxFix 1.2.0 hooks this routine and restores only the suppressed stats 17/18 update for effective nonweapon items; external in-game validation passed for melee and throwable weapon damage on 2026-07-20.

entries.name: STATLIST_MergeStatLists
entries.notes: Merges an item's extended stat list into a target unit. Clearing StatList[0] before calling this routine with mode 1 reproduces the 2.4 charm-aura re-registration path.

entries.name: STATLIST_GetOwner
entries.notes: Returns the owner stored by an item's extended stat list and writes its merge mode. The canonical refresh loop at 0x46F220 calls it before expiring and re-merging each owned inventory item.

entries.name: STATLIST_ExpireUnitStatlist
entries.notes: Canonical stat-list invalidation step called with (player, item) at 0x46F2C4, immediately before STATLIST_MergeStatLists. It clears the stale extended ownership/effects that a direct owner-pointer reset does not cover after death.

entries.name: GetDataTablesForContext
entries.notes: Returns the active compiled data-table container for the classic or expansion context. NoEtherealItemTypes uses it to resolve configured codes against the runtime itemtypes records.

entries.name: DATATBLS_GetItemRecordFromItemCode
entries.notes: Resolves a four-byte item code in the active data context, writes the class ID and returns the corresponding 0x1C0-byte ItemsTxt record. Transmogrify 1.0.3 validates its strict 16-byte entry signature and uses the returned record to obtain the configured output's localized name-string ID.

entries.name: GetItemsTxtRecord
entries.notes: Returns a 0x1C0-byte compiled items record for a data context and class ID. DurabilityResistance 1.1.0 conditionally clears nodurability at +0x122 for bow/xbow families before the caller reads the record.

entries.name: SKILLS_GetBaseLevel
entries.notes: Returns the requested native skill-level view. The skill-tree eligibility path passes flags 0,0 for the base invested level. BulkSkillPointAllocation used it in its superseded mono-rank queue; 1.2.1 no longer polls skill levels.

entries.name: SKILLS_GetSkillById
entries.notes: Resolves a player's skill by skill id and owner GUID. The eligibility path passes owner GUID -1 for the base class skill. BulkSkillPointAllocation used it in its superseded mono-rank queue; 1.2.1 no longer calls it.

entries.name: SKILLS_SetLeftActiveSkill
entries.notes: Canonical left-slot restore routine. It resolves a recreated skill by (skillId, ownerGUID) and stores it at skillList+0x08; CharmInventoryAuras uses it after re-merging aura charms.

entries.name: SKILLS_SetRightActiveSkill
entries.notes: Canonical right-slot restore routine. It resolves a recreated skill by (skillId, ownerGUID) and stores it at skillList+0x10; CharmInventoryAuras uses it after re-merging aura charms.

entries.name: GetItemDataContext
entries.notes: Returns the data-table context byte for an item unit immediately before item-record and item-type lookups.

entries.name: UNITS_GetItemCell
entries.notes: Returns the item's current inventory cell. Transmogrify preserves this value while temporarily detaching a source and uses the output cell when calling the native placement routine.

entries.name: UNITS_GetInventory
entries.notes: Resolves a unit's inventory pointer through the same +A0/+98/+90 reference path as the legacy 0x208330 routine.

entries.name: UNITS_GetInventoryGrid
entries.notes: Maps a player, storage page and expansion flag to the active Inventory.txt grid. Page 0 resolves the main inventory, page 3 the Horadric Cube and page 4 the personal/shared stash grid.

entries.name: UNITS_GetItemData
entries.notes: Returns the item-data pointer stored at unit+0x10. CharmInventoryAuras reads flags at +0x18 and the inventory-grid node position at +0xB8.

entries.name: UNITS_GetSkillList
entries.notes: Returns the skill list stored through the player-unit path at +0x100. Active left and right skill pointers are at skillList+0x08 and +0x10; skill identity is the signed SkillsTxt ID plus owner GUID.

entries.name: UNITS_GetStatListEx
entries.notes: Returns the extended stat-list pointer stored at unit+0x88; its first pointer is the current owning target unit.

entries.name: UNITS_GetUnitType
entries.notes: Returns the unit type used throughout the 92777 stat paths (0 player, 1 monster, 4 item). EnhancedDamageMinMaxFix limits inventory correction to players and monsters.

entries.name: ITEMS_AttachPlacedItem
entries.notes: Performs the native post-placement attachment step for an item and its player. Transmogrify follows the same finalize, attach and conditional refresh sequence as the proven 92777 item-grant path.

entries.name: ITEMS_GetMaxSockets
entries.notes: Returns the maximum socket count for the concrete item. The 92777 implementation reads ItemData item level at +0x38, ItemTypes socket thresholds and the compiled Items gemsockets cap. AdvancedItemTooltips calls this native routine instead of duplicating the rules.

entries.name: ITEMS_CheckItemTypeId
entries.notes: Checks an item against an itemtypes row through D2R's compiled equivalence LUT. The plugin filters only the two ethereal-generation callers.

entries.name: ITEMS_CapturePacketState
entries.notes: Captures the server item's mode, inventory page, packed position and node page into a 16-byte state structure. Transmogrify records this state before emitting the remove-from-container action and detaching the source.

entries.name: ITEMS_InitializePacketState
entries.notes: Initializes the 16-byte item packet state as unplaced (mode 4, inventory page 0xFF, zero position and node). Transmogrify initializes its output state here before setting the selected same-container position.

entries.name: INVENTORY_FindFreePosition
entries.notes: Finds a valid free x/y position for an item in a specified inventory, Inventory.txt grid and storage page. Transmogrify searches only the source container after temporarily freeing the source slot.

entries.name: INVENTORY_GetFirstItem
entries.notes: Validates the 0x01020304 inventory header and returns the first item pointer at inventory+0x10.

entries.name: INVENTORY_RemoveItemFromInventory
entries.notes: Detaches an item from its actual parent inventory and returns the removed unit on success. The ABI and return contract were proven from 92777 call sites and validated by Transmogrify for inventory and stash sources.

entries.name: INVENTORY_GetNextItem
entries.notes: Validates an item unit, resolves ItemData, and returns the linked-list successor at ItemData+0xB0.

entries.name: ITEMS_GetParentInventory
entries.notes: Returns the inventory that currently owns an item. Transmogrify uses it to validate direct player ownership or an item-container whose own parent is the requesting player's inventory.

entries.name: DAMAGE_LogResolvedType
entries.notes: Per-damage-type observer called by SUNITDMG_ApplyResistancesAndAbsorb after resistance and flat reduction are resolved. The 3.2 caller at 0x452651 passes game, attacker, defender, base damage, resistance, reduction, final damage, type name, damage structure, and the table flag. FloatingDamage hooks its build-unique strict 26-byte prologue without modifying any argument or result.

entries.name: PLRMODE_FinalizePlayerMode
entries.notes: D2R 3.2 equivalent of legacy sub_6FC817D0. Its seven-argument ABI and mode-finalization role are proven by the resurrect handler call at 0x4B664B, which passes (game, player, null, TOWNNEUTRAL, 0, 0, 1). CharmInventoryAuras hooks the strict prologue and filters the shared routine by return address 0x4B6650.

entries.name: D2GAME_SKILLMANA_Consume
entries.notes: Native mana and charged-item consumption path used by the official plugin pack.

entries.name: WeaponQuantityDelta
entries.notes: Separate projectile-consumption path for weapon/arrow/bolt/throwable quantity; it is not the melee durability rollover handled by ApplyDurabilityLoss.

entries.name: ITEMS_CreateItemUnit
entries.notes: Creates an item unit from player, class id, game, spawn target, quality, no-sockets, no-ethereal, item level and optional seed arguments. The 11-argument ABI is proven by the 92777 wrapper at 0x517530 and the stack-to-drop-flags mapping in its strict prologue. Transmogrify hooks it only while creating its output and forces noSockets=1 before packet serialization.

entries.name: ITEMS_GetPlayerItemLevel
entries.notes: Returns the native item level selected for a player-created item. Transmogrify uses it when creating an unplaced normal-quality output before same-container placement.

entries.name: SUNIT_RemoveServerUnit
entries.notes: Releases a server unit after it has been detached from inventory ownership. Transmogrify calls it only after the native remove-from-container action and successful inventory detach.

entries.name: ApplyDurabilityLoss
entries.notes: Native durability-loss routine; 4 percent weapon and 10 percent armor thresholds. Throwing weapons used in melee enter this routine, and depleted internal durability can roll over into a one-point stack reduction.

entries.name: ForceMagicPrefixSite
entries.notes: Validated 3.2 equivalent of the legacy force-magic-prefix edit.

entries.name: ForceMagicSuffixSite
entries.notes: Validated 3.2 equivalent of the legacy force-magic-suffix edit.

entries.name: EtherealWeaponTypeGateCall
entries.notes: First ethereal eligibility type check: ITEMS_CheckItemTypeId(item, ITEMTYPE_WEAPON), returning to 0x4432DA.

entries.name: EtherealArmorTypeGateCall
entries.notes: Fallback ethereal eligibility type check: ITEMS_CheckItemTypeId(item, ITEMTYPE_ANY_ARMOR), returning to 0x4432E9.

entries.name: SUNITDMG_ApplyResistancesAndAbsorb
entries.notes: D2R 3.2 equivalent proven from the unique 12-entry damage-stat loop at 0x44EC44, matching damage offsets, resistance/pierce/absorb flow, fixed-point percentage branches, and the post-resolution observer call at 0x452651.

entries.name: ITEMS_FinalizePlacedItem
entries.notes: Finalizes a newly placed server item for its player and game. The native 0x517530 item-grant path calls it after placement and client notification; Transmogrify preserves that sequence.

entries.name: SynchronizeItemAndBoundSkillQuantity
entries.notes: Central routine reached by the Book path; updates STAT_QUANTITY and the bound scroll/book skill.

entries.name: RefreshPlayerItemStatLists
entries.notes: Native full player-item refresh: snapshots vitals and active skills, walks the inventory, obtains each owned item's stat-list mode, calls 0x2F8290 then 0x2F81A0, and restores the snapshot. CharmInventoryAuras invokes it after confirmed corpse recovery and, since 1.6.0, immediately after the softcore player returns to town.

entries.name: ITEMS_RefreshPlayerInventory
entries.notes: Thin native inventory-refresh dispatch used when post-placement attachment reports that a player refresh is required. Transmogrify calls it under the same condition as the proven item-grant path.

entries.name: RejectedJavelinQuantityCandidate
entries.notes: Explicit Javelin item-type path (0x29), not the tome path.

entries.name: ITEMS_PlaceItemForPlayer
entries.notes: Places an item for a player from a 16-byte item state and cell value. Native Cube and generic move call sites prove that the state's inventory page controls the destination; Transmogrify also preserves the source node page for shared-stash routing.

entries.name: CharmAuraZoneTransitionCall
entries.notes: Unique 3.2 equivalent of the legacy 0x2A041F call. Its return address 0x486AE5 selects the intended zone-transition caller of ActTransitionDispatch.

entries.name: SUNIT_GetServerUnit
entries.notes: Resolves a server unit by game, unit type and GUID. Transmogrify uses the item GUID carried by the native use-item packet and requests unit type 4 before any recipe or ownership decision.

entries.name: SUNIT_AttachSound
entries.notes: Stores the 16-bit sound ID and source unit, then schedules the sound. CharmInventoryAuras filters this shared routine by the unique successful corpse-recovery return address 0x4B35A6 and sound ID 0x64.

entries.name: AiSpecialStateDispatchRead
entries.notes: Direct 92777 ABI proof for the AI special state: the AI tick object begins with D2AiControl*, and D2AiControl begins with the signed 32-bit nAiSpecialState. The unique sequence at 0x4A2BC8 loads the tick's first pointer, reads dword [control+0x00] into EDX, calls AITHINK_GetAiTableRecord at 0x4A36C0, then dispatches on the returned record. AISPECIALSTATE_REVIVED remains value 7.

entries.name: AITHINK_GetAiTableRecord
entries.notes: Resolves a unit's active AI table record from nAiSpecialState. Nonzero accepted states index the special-state table at RVA 0x23981F0 with 0x20-byte records; state 7 therefore selects the Revive/NecroPet record at RVA 0x23982D0. Its strict 17-byte prologue is unique in 92777.

entries.name: D2GAME_MONSTERS_AiFunction03
entries.notes: D2R 3.2 generic monster AI preamble containing the inlined equivalent of legacy D2GAME_AICORE_MinionLeash_6FCF0D10. The caller at 0x4A2BBB supplies the game, monster and AI tick state after resolving MonStats and MonStats2; the inlined leash spans 0x4A3B50-0x4A3C6B and matches the legacy owner, walk-mode, owner-life, distance, scatter and catch-up control flow.

entries.name: MinionLeashOwnerScatterGate
entries.notes: Unique 92777 sequence for the legacy close-owner scatter branch: compares full-unit distance with 1, then at 0x4A3C0F requires ownerType UNIT_PLAYER before calling the escape helper at 0x4A7DF0 with distance 19. This path is shared by all applicable minions and must be filtered to AISPECIALSTATE_REVIVED by the plugin instead of patched globally.

entries.name: MinionLeashCatchUpGate
entries.notes: Unique 92777 catch-up sequence matching legacy MinionLeash: compares distance with 20, calls AITACTICS_SetVelocity at 0x4A7270 with mode 7, velocity 0 and bonus 40, then calls the owner-follow helper at 0x4A8090 with distance 19. The exact signature begins 83 F8 14 0F 8E 5A FF FF FF 45 33 C0 41 B1 28.

entries.name: AITACTICS_SetVelocity
entries.notes: Exact 3.2 equivalent of the legacy helper: normalizes mode 1 to 7, validates a monster unit, obtains MonsterData AI velocity parameters and tail-calls the low-level setter with (mode, velocity, byte bonus). MinionLeash calls it at 0x4A3C4A with (monster, 7, 0, 40), returning at 0x4A3C4F; ReviveOverhaul 1.1 uses that exact return address plus the signed call sequence 41 B1 28 48 8B CB 41 8D 50 07 E8 21 36 00 00 to confine velocity configuration to the Revive catch-up path.

entries.name: D2GAME_AICORE_Escape
entries.notes: Escape helper used by the close-owner MinionLeash branch. The call at 0x4A3C29 passes game, minion, owner, distance 19 and flag 1, matching the legacy D2GAME_AICORE_Escape_6FCD0560 invocation.

entries.name: D2GAME_AICORE_WalkToOwner
entries.notes: Owner-follow helper called by both MinionLeash branches at 0x4A3C61 with game, minion, owner and distance 19. Its role and arguments match legacy D2GAME_AICORE_WalkToOwner_6FCD0B60.

entries.name: CorpseRecoverySuccessSoundCall
entries.notes: Unique 18-byte sequence that loads sound 0x64, passes the recovering player as both unit and source, and calls SUNIT_AttachSound after item transfer, corpse-list removal, world deletion and server-unit removal have succeeded. Return address is 0x4B35A6.

entries.name: D2GAME_PACKETCALLBACK_Resurrect
entries.notes: Server handler for the one-byte resurrect packet. The 92777 control flow matches legacy Rcv0x41_Resurrect: validates packet size, requires player mode 0x11 (dead), disconnects hardcore clients, restores life/mana/stamina, changes act, finalizes town-neutral mode, then reassigns both active skills.

entries.name: TownRespawnPlayerModeFinalizeCall
entries.notes: Unique resurrect-handler call to 0x42D2C0 after PlayerChangeAct has returned the softcore player to town. The 43-byte signed setup passes (game, player, null, 1, 0, 0, 1) and returns at 0x4B6650; the hardcore disconnect branch does not reach this site.

entries.name: D2GAME_HandleUseItemPacket
entries.notes: Authoritative server handler for the D2R use-item request. The source item GUID is stored at packet+0x0A in the 92777 packet layout. Transmogrify hooks the strict prologue, validates the resolved item and loaded recipe server-side, and delegates all noneligible items to the original handler.

entries.name: RejectedBookQuantityCandidate
entries.notes: Rejected candidate retained vanilla for regression checks.

entries.name: ActTransitionDispatch
entries.notes: D2R 3.2 equivalent of legacy 0x2EC080. CharmInventoryAuras hooks its build-specific prologue and refreshes charms only when the caller returns to 0x486AE5.

entries.name: ITEMS_GiveQuestItem
entries.notes: Resolves an item code, creates a quality-controlled item through ITEMS_CreateItemUnit, finds player-inventory space, places it and emits the native creation/update packets. Its decompiled 92777 ABI is (game, player, code, itemLevel, quality, groundFallback). It provides the canonical creation/finalization sequence used as the proof model for Transmogrify's explicit same-container placement path.

entries.name: ITEMS_SendItemUpdate
entries.notes: Serializes generalized item actions to the owning client. Native 92777 call sites prove the nine-argument ABI: creation passes action, secondary code, mode, inventory page, node page, then packed position. Transmogrify 1.1.1 follows that order; 1.1.0 had inverted the last two values and produced a visible but frozen client item. Source removal uses action 0x20 with the captured packed position in the final argument.

entries.name: GAME_GetPlayerCountBonus
entries.notes: Computes per-monster player-count bonuses. BKVince hooks it with strict expected bytes to cap monster HP scaling independently at players 8 while the requested difficulty can remain 65535.

entries.name: BookQuantityDeltaBeforeSynchronization
entries.notes: Loads -1 into r9d after the Book type check and before the central quantity synchronization call.

entries.name: AIUTIL_GetDistanceToCoordinates_FullUnitSize
entries.notes: Full-unit-size distance helper used by the inlined MinionLeash at 0x4A3C05. Its result feeds the exact <=1 close-owner and >20 catch-up comparisons, matching the legacy AIUTIL_GetDistanceToCoordinates_FullUnitSize call.

entries.name: LANG_GetStringById
entries.notes: Resolves a 16-bit localized string ID to the active-language UTF-8 text. Independent 92777 callers read the ItemsTxt name-string ID at record offset +0xFC before calling it. Transmogrify 1.0.3 validates its strict 14-byte entry signature and resolves UI string 5387 (convertsto) plus the TMogType output name.

entries.name: INPUT_IsVirtualKeyDownAsync
entries.notes: Thin native wrapper around the imported asynchronous key-state function: it forwards the virtual-key code in ECX and returns bit 15 as a boolean. The skill-tree allocation path calls it with VK_SHIFT at 0x14C69F3. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.0.7 validates the same strict 21-byte function and queries generic, left and right Ctrl; Shift is resolved from the native packet extra marker emitted immediately after this call.

entries.name: SKILLTREE_CanAllocateSkill
entries.notes: Client skill-tree gate taking a skill id and returning a boolean. It obtains the local player, checks unspent skill points stat 5, resolves SkillsTxt and current base level, applies the custom cost/requirement calculation, reads runtime MaxLvl through 0x214220 and rejects blocking state 0x36. The native click path invokes it before BulkSkillPointAllocation observes the 0x3B request; 1.2.1 adds no client-side rank loop.

entries.name: SkillTreeAllocateClickBranch
entries.notes: Skill-tree click branch that extracts the selected skill id and invokes SKILLTREE_CanAllocateSkill before the native 0x3B packet builder.

entries.name: SkillTreeAllocatePacketCall
entries.notes: Validated skill-tree call to CLIENT_SendFiveByteCommandPacket after the eligibility gate. It uses opcode 0x3B, the selected skill id, extra=0 for one total rank, or extra=0xFFFF when Shift requests every permitted rank.

entries.name: StatsAllocateClickHandler
entries.notes: Reference UI handler for stat allocation. Its modifier payload resolves normal click to a total of 1 and Ctrl to 5, then the packet call at 0x14E9F4D passes total-1 in the extra word; Shift takes precedence and follows the confirmation path. This behavior proves the native quantity encoding pattern, but protocol 0x3A itself is not reused for skills.

entries.name: LANG_GetStringByKey
entries.notes: Resolves a UTF-8 string key view {pointer,length} to active-language text. CharacterStatLevelUpButtonWidget_ShowAssignAllConfirmation calls it for the prompt and native Yes/No labels. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 hooks its strict 29-byte prologue and substitutes only AssignAllStatPointsConfirmation while opening its own modal.

entries.name: UI_DispatchMessage
entries.notes: Common UI message dispatcher receiving a message object whose optional payload pointer is stored at +0x110. ConfirmationModal dispatches its selected callback through this entry. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 consumes only the callback carrying its private stat-index sentinel and forwards every other message unchanged.

entries.name: UI_ShowConfirmationModal
entries.notes: Build-92777 ConfirmationModal constructor with ABI (prompt, confirmLabel, dismissLabel, confirmMessage, dismissMessage, displayFlag). It copies the two callback messages into the modal and updates the three text widgets when an existing modal is reused.

entries.name: StatsAllocatePacketCountEncoding
entries.notes: The instruction LEA R8D,[RDI-1] encodes a resolved total allocation count as additional ranks immediately before CLIENT_SendFiveByteCommandPacket. Normal total 1 becomes extra 0 and Ctrl total 5 becomes extra 4. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 applies this proven total-minus-one encoding to opcode 0x3B; extra 4 yielding exactly five instant skill ranks was validated in game on build 92777.

entries.name: CharacterStatLevelUpButtonWidget_ShowAssignAllConfirmation
entries.notes: Reads the stat index at widget+0xB88, constructs confirm and dismiss UI messages, resolves AssignAllStatPointsConfirmation plus native Yes/No labels, and calls UI_ShowConfirmationModal at 0x847C40. BulkSkillPointAllocation 1.2.1 calls this proven builder with a private sentinel instead of reimplementing Diablo's modal layout.
